methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $136M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy

data.narrative:
Aalo Atomics is a Series B nuclear energy company that has raised $136M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear companies including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo. The narrow peer set—same-stage and adjacent-stage nuclear companies—proved insufficient (fewer than 3 datapoints) to support reliable peer-median multiples, requiring reliance on alternative methods.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total capital raised ($136M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression yields $258M. This method is grounded in observed round-to-round dynamics among early-stage frontier tech companies advancing through institutional funding stages.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $220M (low) to $297M (high), with a median point of $258M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the applicability of standard stage-progression multiples to nuclear technology specifically, given the sector's capital intensity and regulatory complexity, and the absence of sufficient close-stage comparables to validate peer-based multiples. Additional financial data on burn rate, capital requirements to next inflection point, or technology-risk metrics would sharpen the analysis.
